ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general bird's nest food product class; no specific ready-to-drink less-sugar packaged variant exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers bird's nest drink as a beverage category. No less-sugar packaged sub-variant code exists; closest parent match documented.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for bird's nest drink. SNOMED CT does not maintain a food composition hierarchy suitable for this product.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single-serve bottle of packaged bird's nest drink is approximately 250 ml (250 g), providing about 68 kcal, 2.9 g protein, and 13.6 g carbohydrate per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Bird's Nest Drink (Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Bird's Nest Drink (Less Sugar, Packaged), CR ID D05010006. All per-100g macronutrient and micronutrient values are taken from this canonical entry with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2008 — sugar-sweetened beverages and analogous lightly sweetened drinks
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for packaged bird's nest drink (less sugar). GI estimated at 52 based on analogous lightly sweetened beverages with comparable sugar content (~5.5 g/100 g) from the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as: GI (52) × available carbohydrate per serving (13.6 g) / 100 = 7.1. This is a low GL. Estimation basis documented per Veritas GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar content (5.45 g/100 g) in a ready-to-drink beverage independently raises blood pressure risk via insulin-mediated sodium retention, despite very low sodium (6 mg/100 g).
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this sugar-sweetened beverage; per AHA 2018 guidelines, added sugar in beverages raises BP risk independently of sodium, and 'less sugar' labelling does not confer cardiovascular safety.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 5.45 g sugar/100 g and negligible dietary fibre (0.1 g/100 g); liquid sugar is absorbed rapidly without fibre modulation, causing acute postprandial glucose excursions.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Per ADA Standards of Medical Care, all sugar-sweetened beverages including 'less sugar' variants should be avoided or strictly limited in type 2 diabetes management; if bird's nest is desired for traditional reasons, prepare at home without added sugar and monitor postprandial blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased CVD risk through dyslipidaemia, insulin resistance, and systemic inflammation; fat content is negligible (0.05 g/100 g) and sodium is very low (6 mg/100 g), but the sugar load remains the primary concern.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should restrict sugar-sweetened beverages including this product; the American Heart Association recommends no more than 450 kcal per week from sugar-sweetened beverages, and this product contributes to that limit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: No anti-inflammatory nutrients present; added sugar contributes to pro-inflammatory mediator production; bird's nest glycoprotein has no documented anti-inflammatory action relevant to RA.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This beverage provides no anti-inflammatory benefit for rheumatoid arthritis and should be replaced with evidence-based anti-inflammatory beverages such as green tea or turmeric-based drinks.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bird's Nest Drink (Less Sugar, Packaged) is a ready-to-drink sweetened beverage commercially produced in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. It is made from water, bird's nest extract derived from the edible nests of swiftlets (primarily Aerodramus fuciphagus), and a reduced quantity of sugar or rock sugar compared to standard formulations. The drink is sold in glass bottles or cans, typically in 250 ml single-serve portions. Nutritionally, it is a low-calorie beverage at 27 kcal per 100 g, with modest protein (1.15 g/100 g) from the bird's nest glycoprotein, very low fat (0.05 g/100 g), and carbohydrate primarily from added sugar (5.45 g/100 g). Dietary fibre is negligible at 0.1 g/100 g and sodium is very low at 6 mg/100 g. Despite the 'less sugar' designation, this product qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage under both ADA and AHA clinical definitions. Bird's nest is traditionally valued in Chinese culture as a tonic food believed to support skin health, immunity, and recovery, though these claims are not substantiated by robust clinical evidence. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 52 (low GI) based on analogous lightly sweetened beverages, but the absence of fibre means postprandial glucose response may be faster than the GI alone suggests. Clinically, this beverage is unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes or hypertension due to its added sugar content. It is halal-certified in most commercial formulations and is gluten-free and dairy-free. Common serving occasions include gifting, post-illness recovery, and as a traditional health tonic during festive periods.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D05010006) with confidence 0.95. This is the canonical approved primary source for Singapore food records. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database, which slightly reduces overall certainty for the GI component, but the nutrient profile confidence remains high.
notes: saturated_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB source; fat_g is 0.05 g/100 g total, with saturated fat below detection threshold — confirmed trace/zero by formulation). monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this packaged bird's nest drink are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D05010006 as of 2026-04-16. calcium_mg and iron_mg are reported as 0.0 per HPB source (confirmed zero). GI value of 52 is an analogous estimate based on lightly sweetened beverages with comparable sugar content from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for packaged bird's nest drink (less sugar) was found in the approved GI source. Glycemic load per 250 g serving = 52 × 13.625 / 100 = 7.1 (low GL). This product is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage under ADA and AHA clinical definitions despite the 'less sugar' label. Bird's nest is not vegetarian or vegan as it is an animal-derived product (swiftlet nest). Halal status applies to most commercial Singapore formulations but consumers should verify individual product certification.
